steps.title: Fastest path: try the choices
steps.content: Because the choices give possible solutions, plug them into the equation instead of solving from scratch. The only choice that satisfies the equation is D, $14$.

steps.title: Isolate the square
steps.content: Start with $k^2-58=138$. Add $58$ to both sides so the square term is alone: $$k^2=196.$$

steps.title: Take both roots
steps.content: If $k^2=196$, then $k=14$ or $k=-14$. The question asks for the positive solution, so keep $14$ and reject $-14$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: D is correct because $14^2-58=196-58=138$. The larger answer choices would make $k^2$ far too large.
